title: Protocol for Making an Animal Model of Blindsight in Macaque Monkeys
author: Takakuwa, Isa, et al.

steps.title: Before you begin
steps.instruction: We use Japanese macaque monkeys (Macaca fuscata) as the model animal for blindsight. In order to use macaque monkeys, it is essential to have a housing environment that strictly fulfills the animal welfare criteria described in the laws or guidelines of your country.

steps.title: Before you begin
steps.instruction: The following guidelines will be helpful when you start breeding primates:

steps.title: Before you begin
steps.instruction: Guide for the Care and Use of Laboratory Animals (https://grants.nih.gov/grants/olaw/guide-for-the-care-and-use-of-laboratory-animals.pdf).

steps.title: Before you begin
steps.instruction: NC3Rs guidelines: non-human primate accommodation, care, and use (https://nc3rs.org.uk/non-human-primate-accommodation-care-and-use).

steps.title: Before you begin
steps.instruction: Guidelines for the Care and Use of Nonhuman Primates of the Japan Neuroscience Society (https://www.jnss.org/en/animal_primates).

steps.title: Before you begin
steps.instruction: Your institutional committee for the animal experimentation is responsible for confirming that your research plan is in accordance with the guidelines in your country or region. The institutional permissions are mandatory for starting your experiments.Note: In our case, all of the experimental procedures were approved by the Committees for Animal Experiments at the Graduate School of Medicine in Kyoto University.

steps.title: Before you begin
steps.instruction: Since macaque monkeys are highly susceptible to tuberculosis, it is desirable for users to take epidemic prevention measures such as confirming that the animals are not affected by tuberculosis with chest X-ray examinations and/or interferon gamma release tests. In addition, it is desirable that the users confirm that the animals have sufficiently high measles antibody levels.

steps.title: Before you begin
steps.instruction: The monkeys have to receive B-virus and simian retrovirus (SRV) antibody inspections. If an animal is kept for longer than 1 year, it is strongly recommended that regular health examinations including tuberculin, dysentery, and salmonella tests are performed.

steps.title: Before you begin
steps.instruction: Newly received animals should be given a period for acclimation after quarantine, before starting the experimental procedures.3

steps.title: Before you begin
steps.instruction: We use a monkey chair when conducting behavioral recordings. Habituation to the monkey chair is required. These steps are largely dependent on your monkey’s housing environment. Please confirm the guidelines of your country and choose suitable environments and methods to meet them.

steps.title: Before you begin
steps.instruction: After satisfying all of these conditions, you can start the experimental protocol that is described in this paper.
